data.title: Bitcoin Gains Recognition as a Store of Value, Says U.S. Treasury Secretary
data.description: According to Foresight News, in an interview with Tucker Carlson, U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent stated that Bitcoin is increasingly being recognized as a store of value. Bessent's comments highlight the growing acceptance of Bitcoin in financial circles as a reliable asset for preserving wealth.

data.title: Story is participating in Binance Phase II voting for listings, and Lianchuang will be a guest in Binance Square live broadcast room tonight
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Story co-founder Jason Zhao will be a guest in the Binance Square live broadcast room at 9:30 pm on April 5th, and will communicate with Binance community users about Story's project progress, AI applications and future trends of programmable IP. Currently, Story is participating in Binance's second round of voting for listings, and its overall ranking has risen to second place.

data.title: Michael Saylor Sends Birthday Wishes to Bitcoin Creator Satoshi Nakamoto
data.description: According to Foresight News, Strategy founder Michael Saylor took to Twitter to extend birthday greetings to Satoshi Nakamoto, the creator of Bitcoin. Saylor's message, "Happy Birthday, Satoshi," celebrates the influential figure behind the cryptocurrency.

data.title: Cathie Wood: Trump's tariffs could push US into recession
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Ark Invest founder and CEO Cathie Wood warned that Trump's new tariffs could push the US economy into a recession. "The market is in considerable turmoil," Cathie Wood said of the uncertainty brought about by these measures. She warned that these policies could push the United States into a recession. "We know how he calculated the reciprocal tariffs, but it doesn't seem to make much sense," she said. "We are in the center of the storm," Wood said. "But if Trump cares about his political legacy - we know he does - or cares about the midterm election campaign season that will begin this fall, then the last period of this rolling recession will give him and the Federal Reserve more freedom to adjust policy." (Thestreet)

data.title: Understanding PSIP: A Key Indicator of Market Sentiment
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the Percent Supply in Profit (PSIP) is a significant on-chain metric that measures the proportion of circulating Bitcoin ($BTC) that is in a profitable state. This indicator is calculated by comparing the last transfer price of each $BTC with its current price. If the current price exceeds the last transfer price, the $BTC is considered to be in profit.

PSIP serves as a guide to market sentiment and has applications in both identifying market tops and bottoms. When the majority of $BTC holdings are at a loss, it often signals a good buying opportunity, as fewer holders are likely to sell at a loss, reducing selling pressure. Conversely, when most holdings are profitable, the market may experience increased selling pressure as holders look to realize gains.

Historically, Bitcoin has shown precise bottoming signals when PSIP falls below 50%, indicating a potential buying opportunity. However, a high PSIP alone does not necessarily indicate a market top, as the maximum value of profitable holdings can reach 100%.

An interesting approach is to observe the correlation between PSIP and price changes, which can provide insights into market dynamics. This concept is further explored in analyses such as 'The Market Pulse – Week 36, 2022.'

For those interested in delving deeper into on-chain data analysis, following this series of articles is recommended. The series aims to provide a comprehensive understanding of on-chain metrics and their implications for market behavior.
